pair_0004.label: same
pair_0004.rationale: Both spans are consecutive Finance Committee sessions (Dec 2022, Jan 2023) tracking the identical specific crisis: the threatened closure of the Lahavim (BTL/Pratt & Whitney compressor blades) plant and the fate of its ~900 workers; the second is explicitly a follow-up session on the same matter.

pair_0096.label: new
pair_0096.rationale: A is about establishing a procedure for appointing Knesset/President's Office/State Comptroller director-generals; B is about extending 'buy Israeli' procurement preference for factories near the Gaza envelope/Sderot. No substantive overlap beyond both being Finance Committee business.

pair_0100.label: new
pair_0100.rationale: A concerns implementation of a public-sector wage agreement for kindergarten teaching assistants; B concerns a bill chapter restricting cash holdings/transactions to combat crime. Entirely different policy matters.

pair_0090.label: new
pair_0090.rationale: A is about war/indirect-damage compensation regulations (Property Tax and Compensation Fund) tied to a specific military escalation; B is a session on mortgage interest rates and bank deposit interest with the Finance Minister. Unrelated subjects.

pair_0041.label: same
pair_0041.rationale: Both sessions (June 20 and July 10, 2023) address the identical, narrowly defined matter: setting the government's special contribution to religious councils' budgets under section 11b of the Jewish Religious Services Law for fiscal year 2023 — a direct continuation of the same funding-formula deliberation.

pair_0076.label: related
pair_0076.rationale: Both spans concern bank interest rates on housing loans vs. deposit interest, part of a recurring committee oversight thread (topic labels nearly identical, B explicitly marked as continued discussion referencing prior sessions). However, the specific sessions differ substantially in framing and scope — A is an MK-petition-driven hearing centered on a comparative rate-gap graph, while B is a broadened session with the Finance Minister tying rates to inflation and business-sector credit access — so I lean toward related rather than merging as one bounded matter, per the same/related tie-break rule.

pair_0031.label: same
pair_0031.rationale: Consecutive-day sessions (May 8 and May 9, 2023) markup the identical chapter of the economic bill on insurance/pension mediation, specifically the same disputed provision on 'unreasonable refusal to contract' between institutional bodies and pension advisors — a direct continuation of the same clause negotiation.

pair_0099.label: new
pair_0099.rationale: A discusses the methodology for the socio-economic index used to extend income-tax benefits for certain settlements; B discusses insurance/pension intermediation legislation (section 36, refusal to contract). Different bills and different substantive matters.

pair_0023.label: same
pair_0023.rationale: Both sessions (April 19 and April 30, 2023) continue markup of the same bill chapter (Chapter D, housing market taxation) within the same economic efficiency law, covering the same set of provisions (rental reporting duty, exemption erosion, housing taxation reform) as an ongoing clause-by-clause negotiation.

pair_0046.label: same
pair_0046.rationale: Both sessions (June 7 and July 16, 2023) concern the identical bill — Sports Gambling Regulation Law Amendment 17 — with the second explicitly following up on negotiations (sports club/center funding terms) opened in the first session.
